transcript.clean_text: onto stage two of parin e with just over 165 km to cover between the brevier and belg slightly flatter than yesterday's opener around sjan onlay but once again it was set to be a brutal day in the saddle with more strong winds forecast on the wide open PLS south of Paris the stage finishing with a triangular circuit into Belgard cheerfully nicknamed the Bermuda Triangle of cycling by one or two in the peltin theoretically another chance for the sprinters including yesterday's stage winner Dylan groven plenty of favorites lost time on stage one and they were soon in trouble again on a fast paced day two into the final 50k and a spectacular crash for raberto Iran luckily no major damage done mik kovski was also held up by a puncture With the Wind causing chaos as expected splintering the field into a number of groups a deela mtal moving to the front with around 25k to go claiming a 1 two3 at the final intermediate Sprint Tony galipan ahead of Roman B and Oliver nassen but several sprinters dropped including Caleb do as were GC hopefuls Simon Yates and Bob jungles Warren barill and gka is air who was third last year both forced to abandon after a crash so a large group coming back together again with 10K to go but still having to resist the cross wins there was then a decisive split late in the day the excellent Luke Roso impressive on his 29th birthday yesterday peeling away with Sky teammates Egan beral and quet kovsky on his wheel seven Riders left at the front and hurling towards the line among them Gran Philip gilber and European Champion Mato Trenton Trenton the first to go but a second straight Victory never looked in out for the Man in the Yellow Jersey groven flying past the Italian and going back to back for yumbo visma at 25 years of age grov vean is emerging as arguably the strongest Sprinter in Road cycling today he becomes the first Dutchman to claim successive stage wins on parin just as he did on stage seven and eight of last just T of France Dylan absolutely glowing in the wind he'll be the hot favorite tomorrow as he looks to complete a hattick two days two victories uh the Jersey is on your shoulders how long do you think you can keep that Jersey on your shoulders uh normal it's tomorrow it's again uh yeah I think a hard stage but also a flat stage so we Tred it again and we go again for a Sprint today it was not the real Sprint but it was a hard stage but yeah I don't know what we can expect to do tomorrow but we will see K back winning stage two comfortably ahead of Ivan Garcia with Jil be just getting his wheel in front of trentin kovski rounding out the top five Simon yat losing almost s minutes while Fabio aru rolled in over 14 minutes back Rovan leads Kat kovsky by 12 seconds overall he also tops the poll in the points classification Bern now the best young Rider is 19 seconds back 4 seconds ahead of Roman B 200k to cover on stage three between sea and muler it's flat a pancake yet with Winds of up to 50 km an hour forecast it'll be another grueling day for the peltin thanks for tuning in and do join us again on Tuesday
